description: Before everyone fishes, there are 100 tons of fish in the lake.
John wanted to catch 10 tons of fish, and caught 10 tons.

The group of fishers, led by John, Kate, Jack, and Emma, agreed to reduce their individual catches from 10 tons to 5 tons next month to ensure the long-term sustainability of the lake's fish population.
conversation: Mayor

Ladies and gentlemen, let me give you the monthly fishing report. John caught 10 tons of fish. Kate caught 10 tons of fish. Jack caught 10 tons of fish. Emma caught 10 tons of fish. Luke caught 10 tons of fish.
John

Hey everyone, it looks like we all caught the same amount of fish this month. We need to think about the long-term sustainability of the lake. If we keep catching 10 tons each, we might deplete the fish population too quickly. Maybe we should discuss a strategy to ensure the fish population remains healthy and we can all continue to benefit.
Kate

I agree with John. If we all keep catching 10 tons each, the fish population might not recover well enough. How about we each reduce our catch to 5 tons next month and see how the fish population responds? This way, we can ensure the lake remains sustainable for all of us in the long run.
Jack

I think Kate's suggestion makes a lot of sense. If we each catch 5 tons next month, we can see how the fish population responds and adjust our strategy accordingly. It's important that we work together to ensure the lake remains sustainable for all of us.
Emma

I agree with Kate and Jack. Reducing our catch to 5 tons each next month is a good idea. It will give us a chance to see how the fish population responds and ensure that we can continue fishing sustainably in the future. Let's all commit to this plan and reassess at the end of next month.

description: I need to remember that Kate, Jack, and Emma have all agreed to reduce their catch to 5 tons each next month to ensure the sustainability of the fish population in the lake

I found it interesting that everyone quickly agreed to reduce their catch to 5 tons each next month, showing a collective willingness to prioritize the long-term sustainability of the lake over short-term individual gains
The community agreed on a maximum limit of 5 tons of fish per person.
